title: First ride out — why I bought a Honda
excerpt: It started with a busted up CB350 in someone's front yard and a handwritten 'for sale' sign. I should've kept driving.
content:
It started with a busted up CB350 in someone's front yard and a handwritten 'for sale' sign. I should've kept driving.

I didn't.

There's something about an old Honda that gets under your skin. They're not glamorous. They're not loud. They don't turn heads at bike shows. But they run. They always run. You kick them once, maybe twice, and they just... go.

I threw a leg over it in the driveway. The seat was cracked. The mirrors were wrong. The tank had a dent the size of a fist. The guy selling it looked at me like he wasn't sure whether to be grateful or suspicious.

I paid cash. Rode it home. It only died twice.

That was six months ago. Now I can't imagine not having it.
